answer:
**Yājñavalkya Smṛti, Chapter 3 ("ON EXPIATION (prāyaścittādhyāya)"),** contains this precise anatomical inventory of a man's body amid a broader discussion of the body's impermanence and constitution to encourage detachment.[^1]

This matches all specified numbers: three hundred and sixty bones;[^2] sixty-four teeth with their sockets; twenty nails; seventy-two ribs (with their sockets and nodules); forty-five bones in the back; fifteen in the neck; seventeen in the chest;[^3] seven hundred veins, nine hundred sinews, two hundred arteries, and five hundred muscles;[^4] one hundred and seven vital spots and two hundred joints; and fifty crores and four crores [...] body hairs [...] and sixty-seven and a half lakhs, along with the sweat ducts.[^5]

[^1]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, [Chapter 3](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/03/translation/).

[^2]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, *Chapter 3*, [// Yj_3.84](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/03/translation/#p-ac1be2): "His bodies are sixfold, and six skins support them; there are six limbs, **and three hundred and sixty bones.** // Yj_3.84 //"

[^3]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, *Chapter 3*, [paragraphs](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/03/translation/#p-c7a123) (Yj_3.88: back, neck) through [// Yj_3.90](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/03/translation/#p-8c0993) (chest).

[^4]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, *Chapter 3*, [// Yj_3.100](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/03/translation/#p-e42749): "**Seven hundred veins, nine hundred sinews, two hundred arteries, and five hundred muscles.** // Yj_3.100 //"

[^5]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, *Chapter 3*, [// Yj_3.103](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/03/translation/#p-b7896b).

args.query: "fifty crores" OR "four crores" body OR veins OR hairs
